cmd/balance-worker.purpose: Binary entrypoint for the balance-worker: subscribes to Kafka system and ingest topics via Watermill, recalculates metered entitlement grant burn-down, and dispatches balance threshold notifications. All business logic lives in openmeter/entitlement/balanceworker; this folder only wires and runs it.

patterns.name: Wire-only entrypoint
patterns.description: wire.go declares the Application struct and wire.Build call; wire_gen.go is generated. main.go only calls initializeApplication, app.SetGlobals(), app.Migrate(), app.Run(). No business logic in main.go.
patterns.example: app, cleanup, err := initializeApplication(ctx, conf); defer cleanup(); app.SetGlobals(); app.Run()

patterns.name: Shared Application interface via embedding
patterns.description: Application embeds common.GlobalInitializer, common.Migrator, and common.Runner — all lifecycle methods come from those shared structs, not defined locally.
patterns.example: type Application struct { common.GlobalInitializer; common.Migrator; common.Runner; Logger *slog.Logger; ... }

patterns.name: Config extraction via wire.FieldsOf
patterns.description: Worker-specific sub-config slices are extracted via wire.FieldsOf before passing to provider sets: wire.FieldsOf(new(config.Configuration), "BalanceWorker") and wire.FieldsOf(new(config.BalanceWorkerConfiguration), "ConsumerConfiguration").
patterns.example: wire.FieldsOf(new(config.BalanceWorkerConfiguration), "ConsumerConfiguration")

patterns.name: Metadata function per binary
patterns.description: Each binary defines a local metadata() function passing version and the binary name string to common.NewMetadata. The name string appears in telemetry labels.
patterns.example: func metadata(conf config.Configuration) common.Metadata { return common.NewMetadata(conf, version, "balance-worker") }

patterns.name: Version via ldflags + runtime/debug fallback
patterns.description: version/revision/revisionDate are provisioned by ldflags. init() falls back to debug.ReadBuildInfo() vcs settings when ldflags are absent.
patterns.example: var version string; func init() { if version == "" { version = "unknown" } }

key_file_guides.role: Declares Application struct fields and lists all common.* provider sets passed to wire.Build. Edit here when adding a new service dependency to the binary.
key_file_guides.watch_for: wire.go has build tag //go:build wireinject — never remove it. wire_gen.go has //go:build !wireinject — never edit it manually.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by `go generate` (wire). Contains the full initializeApplication function. DO NOT EDIT.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any manual edit will be overwritten on next `make generate`.

key_file_guides.role: Parses pflag/viper config, calls initializeApplication, and invokes lifecycle methods. Calls app.Migrate(ctx) before app.Run().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add business logic here. Keep startup sequence: parse flags → unmarshal config → validate → initializeApplication → SetGlobals → Migrate → Run.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic to main.go instead of openmeter/entitlement/balanceworker
Manually editing wire_gen.go
Removing //go:build wireinject tag from wire.go
Adding a new domain service dependency without adding its provider set to wire.Build in wire.go
Introducing context.Background() for anything other than the root ctx at program start

decisions.decision: common.Runner encapsulates the oklog/run group; main.go never builds run.Group directly.
decisions.rationale: Keeps shutdown ordering, signal handling, and goroutine lifecycle consistent across all workers without duplicating boilerplate.

decisions.decision: BalanceWorker depends on Notification and Subject services even though it does not serve HTTP.
decisions.rationale: Balance threshold events are dispatched to notification.Service after burn-down recalculation, requiring Svix plumbing wired here.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new provider set to the balance-worker DI graph

cmd/benthos-collector.purpose: Binary entrypoint for the Redpanda Benthos/Connect collector: registers custom OpenMeter bloblang, input, and output plugins via blank imports, then delegates entirely to service.RunCLI with optional leader-election CLI opts. No Wire DI — no domain services are wired here.

patterns.name: Plugin registration via blank imports
patterns.description: All OpenMeter Benthos extensions (bloblang functions, custom inputs, custom outputs) are registered by blank-importing their packages before calling service.RunCLI.
patterns.example:
import _ "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/collector/benthos/bloblang"
import _ "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/collector/benthos/input"
import _ "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/collector/benthos/output"

patterns.name: Minimal main — delegate to Benthos RunCLI
patterns.description: main() creates a cancellable context, appends leaderelection CLI opts, and calls service.RunCLI. All pipeline logic is in YAML config files, not Go.
patterns.example: service.RunCLI(ctx, leaderelection.GetLeaderElectionCLIOpts(ctx)...)

patterns.name: Simplified version init (no runtime/debug)
patterns.description: version.go only sets version = "unknown" if unset via ldflags; no vcs revision tracking unlike other binaries.
patterns.example: func init() { if version == "" { version = "unknown" } }

key_file_guides.role: Entry point: registers plugins, starts Benthos CLI with leader election opts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New plugins must be blank-imported here to be registered before RunCLI is called; plugins not imported will silently not appear in the Benthos component registry.

anti_patterns: Adding Wire DI or domain service instantiation — all pipeline logic belongs in collector/benthos
Adding Kafka consumer/producer Go code directly — use Benthos YAML pipeline configs instead
Forgetting blank imports when adding new bloblang/input/output plugins in collector/benthos/*

decisions.decision: No Wire DI: this binary is a thin launcher for the Benthos framework, not a Go service.
decisions.rationale: Benthos manages its own plugin/component registry and YAML-driven pipeline wiring; injecting Go DI would duplicate what Bentmill already provides.

cmd/billing-worker.purpose: Binary entrypoint for the billing-worker: subscribes to Kafka system events via Watermill, processes subscription sync, invoice creation, and charge advancement events. Performs post-migration provisioning (ledger business accounts, sandbox app) before starting the run loop.

patterns.name: Post-migration provisioning before Run
patterns.description: billing-worker calls app.LedgerAccountResolver.EnsureBusinessAccounts and app.AppRegistry.SandboxProvisioner after Migrate and before Run — ordering is critical for correct startup state.
patterns.example:
app.Migrate(ctx)
app.LedgerAccountResolver.EnsureBusinessAccounts(ctx, namespace)
app.AppRegistry.SandboxProvisioner(ctx, namespace)
app.Run()

patterns.name: Wire-only entrypoint with extended Application struct
patterns.description: Application embeds common.GlobalInitializer, Migrator, Runner and exposes AppRegistry, LedgerAccountResolver, NamespaceManager, Streaming as fields needed for post-migration provisioning in main.go.
patterns.example: type Application struct { common.GlobalInitializer; common.Migrator; common.Runner; AppRegistry common.AppRegistry; LedgerAccountResolver ledger.AccountResolver; NamespaceManager *namespace.Manager; ... }

patterns.name: BillingWorker provider set centralises complex billing wiring
patterns.description: wire.Build uses common.BillingWorker which internally wires billing adapter, rating service, subscription sync, charges, and ledger stack. Individual billing sub-services are not listed separately in wire.Build.
patterns.example: common.BillingWorker // in wire.Build — provisions billingRegistry, subscriptionsyncService, workerOptions, worker in sequence

patterns.name: Config FieldsOf extraction for worker sub-config
patterns.description: wire.FieldsOf(new(config.BillingWorkerConfiguration), "ConsumerConfiguration") and wire.FieldsOf(new(config.BillingConfiguration), "Worker") extract the router/consumer config slices for Wire.
patterns.example: wire.FieldsOf(new(config.BillingWorkerConfiguration), "ConsumerConfiguration")

key_file_guides.role: Startup sequence: parse config → initializeApplication → SetGlobals → Migrate → EnsureBusinessAccounts → SandboxProvisioner → Run.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EnsureBusinessAccounts and SandboxProvisioner must run after Migrate but before Run. Adding new post-migration provisioning must follow this order.

key_file_guides.role: Declares Application struct and all provider sets. Edit here to add new service dependencies.
key_file_guides.watch_for: common.BillingWorker is a composite set — don't duplicate the billing sub-services it already includes.

key_file_guides.role: Generated — DO NOT EDIT. Shows full wiring sequence including ledger stack, app registry, Stripe/CustomInvoicing/Sandbox apps.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Ledger stack wiring (NewLedgerHistoricalRepo, NewLedgerAccountService, etc.) is guarded by creditsConfiguration — verify credits path when debugging ledger writes.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic to main.go — it belongs in openmeter/billing/worker or openmeter/billing/worker/subscriptionsync
Calling EnsureBusinessAccounts or SandboxProvisioner after Run() — they must execute before the worker starts consuming events
Manually editing wire_gen.go
Duplicating billing sub-service provider sets already included by common.BillingWorker
Bypassing the credits.enabled guard by directly constructing ledger services without common.LedgerStack

decisions.decision: Ledger business accounts and sandbox app are provisioned in billing-worker (not only in cmd/server) to support standalone billing-worker deployments.
decisions.rationale: When billing-worker runs independently of cmd/server, it must self-provision its required DB state before consuming Kafka events.

decisions.decision: AppRegistry (Sandbox, Stripe, CustomInvoicing) is wired in billing-worker because invoice lifecycle events require the app implementations to be registered before handling.
decisions.rationale: Invoice state machine dispatches to the registered InvoicingApp; wiring must happen before the Watermill router starts consuming events.

code_examples.scenario: Correct post-Migrate startup sequence in billing-worker main.go

cmd/notification-service.purpose: Binary entrypoint for the notification-service: wires domain services via Wire, then manually constructs the Watermill Kafka subscriber and notification consumer in main.go and runs them in an oklog/run group. Unlike other workers, the Watermill subscriber and consumer.New call are in main.go, not delegated to a common.Runner.

patterns.name: Manual run group assembly in main.go
patterns.description: notification-service builds its own run.Group in main.go (telemetry server + notificationConsumer.Run + signal handler) rather than using common.Runner. The Application struct exposes BrokerOptions, MessagePublisher, Meter, Tracer, TelemetryServer for assembly in main.go.
patterns.example: group.Add(func() error { return notificationConsumer.Run(ctx) }, func(err error) { _ = notificationConsumer.Close() })

patterns.name: Subscriber constructed in main.go, not in Wire
patterns.description: watermillkafka.NewSubscriber is called in main.go using app.BrokerOptions and conf.Notification.Consumer.ConsumerGroupName — the subscriber is not part of the Wire graph.
patterns.example: wmSubscriber, err := watermillkafka.NewSubscriber(watermillkafka.SubscriberOptions{ Broker: app.BrokerOptions, ConsumerGroupName: conf.Notification.Consumer.ConsumerGroupName })

patterns.name: consumer.Options wires app fields to consumer
patterns.description: consumer.Options{} aggregates SystemEventsTopic, Router (router.Options with subscriber/publisher/logger/meter/tracer), Marshaler, and Notification service from app fields.
patterns.example: consumer.Options{ SystemEventsTopic: conf.Events.SystemEvents.Topic, Router: router.Options{Subscriber: wmSubscriber, Publisher: app.MessagePublisher, ...}, Notification: app.Notification }

patterns.name: Application exposes raw primitives (BrokerOptions, Meter, Tracer) as fields
patterns.description: Unlike other workers that embed common.Runner, notification-service Application exposes metric.Meter, trace.Tracer, message.Publisher directly so main.go can assemble consumer.Options.
patterns.example: type Application struct { ... BrokerOptions watermillkafka.BrokerOptions; MessagePublisher message.Publisher; Meter metric.Meter; Tracer trace.Tracer; ... }

key_file_guides.role: Builds Kafka subscriber, constructs consumer.Options, instantiates consumer.New, assembles and runs the oklog run.Group. This is intentionally more manual than other workers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: consumer.Options.Marshaler must be app.EventPublisher.Marshaler() — mismatching marshalers causes message deserialization failures.

key_file_guides.role: Application struct exposes more raw primitives than other workers (BrokerOptions, MessagePublisher, Meter, Tracer) because main.go needs them for manual consumer construction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: common.NotificationServiceProvisionTopics must be listed to ensure the correct Kafka topic provisioning for notification-service.

key_file_guides.role: Generated — DO NOT EDIT. Note: TelemetryServer is a field on Application (not on common.Runner) because notification-service manages it in its own run.Group.
key_file_guides.watch_for: StreamingConnector is wired even though notification-service doesn't query usage — it's a transitive dependency of the notification service chain.

anti_patterns: Moving Kafka subscriber construction into Wire — it is intentionally in main.go so consumer group name can come from config at startup
Using app.MessagePublisher as the eventbus.Publisher for marshaling — use app.EventPublisher.Marshaler() for event deserialization
Adding business notification logic to main.go — it belongs in openmeter/notification/consumer
Manually editing wire_gen.go

decisions.decision: notification-service constructs its Watermill subscriber in main.go rather than Wire.
decisions.rationale: The consumer group name is a runtime config value tied to deployment identity; keeping subscriber construction in main.go makes the config-to-subscriber mapping explicit and avoids parameterising Wire with runtime strings.

code_examples.scenario: Assembling notification consumer in main.go after Wire initialization

cmd/server.purpose: Binary entrypoint for the main HTTP API server: wires all domain services via Wire, runs DB migrations, registers namespace handlers in strict order (Ledger then KafkaIngest before initNamespace), provisions sandbox app and default billing profile, constructs router.Config with all services, and runs an oklog/run group with API server, telemetry server, Kafka producer, and notification event handler.

patterns.name: Namespace handler registration before initNamespace
patterns.description: LedgerNamespaceHandler and KafkaIngestNamespaceHandler MUST be registered on NamespaceManager before initNamespace is called — otherwise the default namespace is provisioned without those handlers.
patterns.example:
app.NamespaceManager.RegisterHandler(app.LedgerNamespaceHandler)
app.NamespaceManager.RegisterHandler(app.KafkaIngestNamespaceHandler)
initNamespace(app.NamespaceManager, logger)

patterns.name: router.Config aggregates all domain services
patterns.description: server.NewServer receives a router.Config struct with every domain service as a named field. Adding a new endpoint requires adding the service to router.Config and wiring it in Application/wire.go.
patterns.example: router.Config{ Billing: app.BillingRegistry.Billing, Customer: app.Customer, Notification: app.Notification, ChargeService: app.BillingRegistry.ChargesServiceOrNil(), ... }

patterns.name: Run group with four components
patterns.description: main.go assembles a run.Group with: telemetry server, Kafka producer (kafkaingest.KafkaProducerGroup), API HTTP server, notification event handler, termination checker, and signal handler — all with graceful shutdown.
patterns.example:
group.Add(kafkaingest.KafkaProducerGroup(ctx, app.KafkaProducer, logger, app.KafkaMetrics))
group.Add(apiServerRun, apiServerShutdown)
group.Add(eventHandlerStart, eventHandleStop)

patterns.name: Post-migration provisioning before server start
patterns.description: After Migrate: register namespace handlers, initNamespace, SandboxProvisioner, ProvisionDefaultBillingProfile, MeterConfigInitializer — all must succeed before server.NewServer and run.Group start.
patterns.example:
app.BillingRegistry.Billing.ProvisionDefaultBillingProfile(ctx, namespace)
app.MeterConfigInitializer(ctx)

patterns.name: Largest Application struct in codebase
patterns.description: cmd/server Application exposes ~40 fields covering every domain service (Addon, AppRegistry, BillingRegistry, CostService, CreditGrantService, Ledger, EntitlementRegistry, KafkaProducer, NotificationEventHandler, etc.) — all declared in wire.go.
patterns.example: type Application struct { common.GlobalInitializer; common.Migrator; Addon addon.Service; AppRegistry common.AppRegistry; BillingRegistry common.BillingRegistry; ... }

key_file_guides.role: Orchestrates full startup: config parse → Wire init → SetGlobals → Migrate → namespace handler registration → initNamespace → provisioning → server construction → run group.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new namespace.Handler must be registered BEFORE initNamespace(). Any new post-migration provisioning step must happen before the run.Group starts.

key_file_guides.role: Largest Wire file: lists ~30 provider sets including common.App, common.Billing, common.LedgerStack, common.Kafka, common.KafkaIngest, common.Server. Add new service dependencies here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: common.LedgerStack is the credits-aware ledger provider — don't construct ledger services outside it. common.WatermillNoPublisher (not common.Watermill) is used because server publishes but does not consume Kafka events.

key_file_guides.role: Generated — DO NOT EDIT. The longest generated file (~600 lines of constructor calls). Reference to understand dependency order.
key_file_guides.watch_for: credits-guarded ledger construction appears here — verify noop vs real implementation when debugging credits.enabled=false paths.

anti_patterns: Registering a namespace.Handler after initNamespace — the default namespace will be missing that handler's provisioning
Adding domain service calls directly in main.go startup sequence without error handling and os.Exit(1)
Manually editing wire_gen.go
Adding a new HTTP endpoint without adding its service to router.Config and declaring it in wire.go Application struct
Using common.Watermill instead of common.WatermillNoPublisher — server does not consume Kafka topics

decisions.decision: cmd/server is the only binary that registers namespace handlers; other workers don't register Kafka ingest or ledger namespace handlers.
decisions.rationale: Namespace provisioning (ClickHouse table creation, Kafka topic creation, ledger account setup) must happen exactly once via the server startup path.

decisions.decision: NotificationEventHandler is run as a goroutine in cmd/server's run.Group, not in a separate binary.
decisions.rationale: The notification event handler is lightweight and co-located with the API server to simplify deployment; a dedicated notification-service binary is also available for horizontal scaling.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new domain service to cmd/server and its HTTP handler
